purpose: Define the implementation directions, database structures, APIs, scripts, pass/fail gates, fallbacks, and AI/data standards required to upgrade Fitness Hub AI into a global food logger with strong India-aware intelligence and Hevy-grade workout logging.

productPositioning.primaryMarketContext: India-first usage context, globally capable food and workout logging.
foodScope: Western and global meals remain first-class: pizza, pasta, burgers, oats, whey, salads, sandwiches, restaurant meals, packaged foods.
Indian foods are also first-class: thalis, dal, sabzi, curries, rice dishes, rotis, dosa, idli, poha, biryani, paneer dishes, chaat, street food, sweets, regional cuisines, homemade meals.
The system must support mixed modern diets where the same user logs Indian home food, Western restaurant food, packaged snacks, supplements, and gym-focused high-protein meals.
measurementScope: Metric units are default: grams, milliliters, kilograms, centimeters.
Indian household portions are supported through conversion metadata: katori, roti, chapati, phulka, bowl, plate, glass, piece, ladle, spoon, cup, serving.
Global units are accepted where users expect them: cup, ounce, slice, scoop, serving.
workoutScope: Workout logging must work in low-network gym environments through local-first state and later sync.
Exercise naming should support Indian gym usage, common machine names, custom exercises, and globally standard exercise taxonomy.

security: No provider keys, database credentials, Clerk secrets, Gemini secrets, bearer tokens, or service-role keys may be exposed to frontend code.
All provider-backed food lookup, label extraction, and AI analysis must run through API-server routes or server-side integration packages.
Admin, trainer, and member access must continue to be enforced by Clerk-gated API middleware.
AI features are advisory and draft-based. No nutrition or workout plan mutation happens without explicit user action.
database: Use migrations only. Do not modify production schema directly.
Migrations must be idempotent and safe to re-run where possible.
All new public-facing IDs must be UUID-compatible text IDs.
All new tables require created_at and updated_at timestamps.
Indexes must exist for columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY, sync, lookup, or history queries.
Related writes must be wrapped in transactions.
Production migration execution requires backup confirmation first.

If the production database is Supabase Postgres and Clerk/Supabase JWT claims are mapped, enable RLS on new tables and add gym/member-scoped policies. If claims are not mapped, block RLS rollout until auth mapping is verified to avoid breaking API writes.
frontend: TypeScript strict mode must remain green.
Stateful member logging logic belongs in hooks/contexts/helpers, not large UI-only components.
All logging flows must include loading, empty, error, and offline/fallback states.
Interactive controls must be keyboard reachable where web-previewable.
Do not introduce visual clutter or marketing-shell screens.
ai: AI outputs must include confidence, provenance, prompt version, model version, and editable draft fields.
Low-confidence AI results must route users toward correction instead of silent auto-save.
User corrections are data signals and must be stored in structured form.
AI memory stores durable patterns only, not raw noisy daily logs or raw food images.

baseline.name: root_typecheck
baseline.fail: Any TypeScript error, generated contract drift, missing import, or invalid type.

baseline.name: gymapp_tests
baseline.pass: All member app tests pass, including nutrition/workout history helpers.
baseline.fail: Any helper, context, storage, or UI logic regression.

baseline.name: api_logging_tests
baseline.command: pnpm --dir artifacts/api-server test -- tests/routes/nutrition.test.mjs tests/routes/workouts.test.mjs tests/routes/ai.test.mjs tests/routes/ai-quality.test.mjs
baseline.pass: Nutrition, workout, AI, and AI quality route tests pass.
baseline.fail: Any route behavior, validation, auth, persistence, or AI safety regression.

contractAndCodegen.name: openapi_codegen
contractAndCodegen.pass: Generated React Query and Zod clients match OpenAPI source.
contractAndCodegen.fail: Generated files change unexpectedly after a supposedly complete implementation, or codegen exits non-zero.

contractAndCodegen.name: generated_drift_check
contractAndCodegen.pass: No unreviewed generated drift remains after codegen.
contractAndCodegen.fail: Generated output differs from committed source-of-truth changes.

database.name: db_schema_typecheck
database.pass: Drizzle schema and shared package types compile.
database.fail: Any schema export, inferred type, or shared type failure.

database.name: migration_review
database.pass: No destructive migration pattern appears unless explicitly approved and documented.
database.fail: Unapproved destructive migration statement is present.

uiAndRelease.name: ui_preview
uiAndRelease.command: CI=1 pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/ui-preview.spec.ts
uiAndRelease.pass: Admin/member preview states render without visual/logging regressions.
uiAndRelease.fail: Any critical preview state fails, overlaps, or blocks logging.

uiAndRelease.name: smoke
uiAndRelease.pass: Admin/API smoke test passes with available local services.
uiAndRelease.fail: Auth, API base URL, admin shell, or API smoke behavior fails.

uiAndRelease.name: internal_beta_gate
uiAndRelease.command: mise exec node@22 -- pnpm run verify:internal-beta
uiAndRelease.pass: Node preflight, secret preflight, codegen, API tests, build, and Playwright checks pass.
uiAndRelease.fail: Any internal beta gate command fails. If blocked by credentials or external provider setup, record exact blocker and do not call release-ready.

nutrition.purpose: Server-side food/product cache for provider-backed and curated foods.
columns: created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
indexes: unique(source, source_product_id) where source_product_id is not null
index(barcode) where barcode is not null
notes: Portion options must include base grams/ml conversion.
Indian household portions are stored here, not hardcoded in UI.

nutrition.purpose: Member-specific custom foods, corrections, favorites, and personal defaults.
columns: is_favorite boolean not null default false
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

nutrition.purpose: Provider, AI, barcode, label, and correction event log for reliability and accuracy evaluation.
columns: created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
indexes: index(barcode) where barcode is not null
nutrition.privacy: Store hashes and structured metadata only. Do not store raw food images by default.

workouts.purpose: Canonical exercise library for global gym movements and Indian gym naming aliases.
columns: is_system boolean not null default true
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

workouts.purpose: Custom exercises created by members or trainers without breaking history.
columns: created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

workouts.purpose: Derived normalized set history for fast exercise history, progression, analytics, and PRs.
columns: completed boolean not null default false
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
workouts.writePolicy: Rebuild derived rows transactionally whenever a workout session is created or updated.

workouts.purpose: Metric-specific PR records instead of a single one-size-fits-all personal record.
columns: created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

aiDataInfrastructure.purpose: Track prompt/model versions used for food analysis, label analysis, workout suggestions, and memory updates.
columns: is_active boolean not null default false
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()

aiDataInfrastructure.purpose: Structured telemetry for quality, latency, confidence, fallback, and correction analysis.
columns: user_corrected boolean not null default false
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
aiDataInfrastructure.privacy: Use input hashes and structured summaries. Do not persist raw images or full raw prompts unless explicitly approved for a secure eval dataset.

apiPlan.purpose: Search member custom foods, curated catalog, Open Food Facts cached products, and USDA-backed foods.
passCriteria: Never returns secrets or raw provider credentials.
Returns provenance and portion options.
Handles Indian aliases and global food terms.

apiPlan.purpose: Resolve packaged food barcode to nutrition facts.
passCriteria: Cached hit is fast.
Provider miss does not block manual logging.
Lookup event records provider status and latency.

apiPlan.purpose: Save corrected/custom foods and user defaults.
passCriteria: Validates finite non-negative macros.
Persists serving grams or explicit conversion gap.
Stores source and confidence.

apiPlan.purpose: Extract structured nutrition from a package label image.
passCriteria: Returns editable draft only.
Includes prompt version, model, confidence, and fields needing review.
Rejects oversized images.
Search is fast enough for live workout use.
Aliases and equipment filters work.
Archived custom exercises do not appear by default.

apiPlan.purpose: Return previous sets, PRs, volume trend, and progression context for an exercise.
passCriteria: Uses member_workout_sets indexes.
Returns completed set history only.
Does not leak other members' data.

apiPlan.purpose: Return volume, consistency, muscle distribution, and PR summaries.
passCriteria: Bounded queries with date range.
No unindexed full history scans for common views.

nutritionLookupCascade: Use member custom/favorite/recent food if exact or high-similarity match exists.
Use cached food_catalog_items by barcode or source_product_id.
Use Open Food Facts for packaged barcode/product data.
Use USDA FoodData Central for generic/global nutrition details when provider coverage is better.
Use India-aware curated catalog and alias matching for common Indian foods and portions.
Use AI photo or label analysis as an editable draft with confidence.
Use manual entry as the always-available final fallback.
workoutCascade: Use local active session and AsyncStorage first.
Use normalized server history when available.
Use local completed session history when offline.
Use exercise catalog defaults when no user history exists.
Allow custom exercise creation when catalog search fails.
aiCascade: Provider model returns valid structured JSON.
If JSON parse fails, retry once with repair prompt server-side.
If confidence is low or required fields are missing, return manual draft state.
If AI provider is unavailable, preserve user intent and continue with manual logging.
syncCascade: Server sync attempts when auth and API base are available.
Failed sync is queued or retried without deleting local state.
Conflicts are surfaced when entry-level sync exists; until then, latest user-confirmed daily log wins.

dataModel: Every food and workout AI output has provenance, confidence, model, prompt version, and schema version.
Every user correction creates a structured signal for future defaults and evaluation.
AI memory stores durable behavior patterns, not raw logs.
Provider and AI failure telemetry is queryable by task, latency, status, and fallback.
evaluation: Create a small India-aware eval set for food photos and text entries covering mixed Indian/global diets.
Include portion edge cases: katori dal, two rotis, half plate biryani, scoop whey, slice pizza, cafe sandwich, packaged snack.
Track macro error against corrected user values when user edits are available.
Track correction frequency by food type, provider, prompt version, and confidence.
prompting: Food prompts must explicitly understand Indian and global foods.
Prompts must return uncertainty instead of overconfident estimates.
Label prompts must distinguish per-serving and per-100g nutrition.
Workout prompts must use history as context but never auto-mutate routines.
privacy: Do not store raw images by default.
Use hashes and structured metadata for repeated lookup and evaluation.
Any future secure eval dataset requires explicit approval and retention policy.

implementationWaves.title: Baseline and Branch Hygiene
directions: Do not mix the current staged V1 implementation with the new database/API architecture without a checkpoint.
Run baseline verification before new schema work.
Record any existing blocker before touching code.
pass: Current staged work is understood.
Baseline tests either pass or exact pre-existing blockers are documented.
fail: New changes are added without knowing whether the current branch is already green.

implementationWaves.title: Database Foundations
directions: Add Drizzle schema exports and one idempotent migration for the new food, exercise, derived set, PR, and AI telemetry tables.
Do not seed catalog data in migrations.
Add indexes with the migration, not later.
pass: Schema compiles.
Migration has no destructive operations.
All new tables have timestamps and UUID-compatible IDs.
Indexes cover lookup and history queries.
fail: Missing indexes for barcode, exercise history, or member date-range access.
RLS is enabled without verified auth claims and breaks API writes.

implementationWaves.title: API and Provider Layer
directions: Update OpenAPI first, then regenerate clients.
Cache provider results and record lookup events.
Add strict validation for nutrition entries and workout history queries.
pass: Endpoints are Clerk-gated.
Provider misses fall back to manual draft.
OpenAPI codegen and typecheck pass.
Route tests cover success, miss, invalid payload, auth, and provider failure.

implementationWaves.title: Member Logging UX
directions: Upgrade food logging without removing manual entry.
Upgrade workout history without slowing active set logging.
pass: Photo, barcode, search, recent, favorite, and manual food flows are visible and recoverable.
Exercise search, custom exercise, previous sets, and history are visible during workout logging.
Low-confidence AI and provider failures have clear correction states.
fail: AI or network failure prevents basic logging.
Workout set logging becomes slower or visually crowded.
Indian/global portion conversions are ambiguous at save time.

implementationWaves.title: AI Data Maturity
directions: Add eval fixtures before claiming AI quality improvement.
pass: AI outputs are auditable by prompt/model/schema version.
Correction signals can be queried.
Raw images are not stored by default.
Fallback reasons are recorded.
fail: AI quality claims are made without eval data.
Raw private inputs are stored without approval.
AI memory stores noisy raw logs.

implementationWaves.title: Verification and Release Candidate
directions: Run targeted tests first, then full internal beta gate.
Do not call the feature complete if provider credentials or migrations are unverified.
pass: Targeted API and gymapp tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
UI previews pass.
Internal beta gate passes or exact external blocker is documented.
fail: Skipping OpenAPI codegen after contract changes.
Ignoring an auth, secret, provider, or migration blocker.

canShipInternalBeta: All baseline and release scripts pass.
No secrets are exposed to frontend bundles.
New DB tables are migration-backed and indexed.
Food and workout logging preserve source/provenance/history.
mustNotShip: AI auto-saves uncertain nutrition without user review.
Workout sessions or PRs can be written outside the current member/gym scope.
Barcode or AI provider outage blocks logging.
Production migration backup/RLS/auth-readiness is not addressed.
